steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says biophilic design uses organic or biomorphic forms to connect buildings with nature, then uses therefore to explain why curves fit this approach. We need the choice that explains curves through their resemblance to natural forms.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — are reminiscent of the arcing and contours found among plants and animals.: curves mirror the arcing contours found in plants and animals, making them a natural fit for biomorphic design that seeks to echo living forms. ✓
  • Choice B — are common in building designs in many different regions and cultures.: says curves are common in architecture generally—does not connect curves to nature or biophilic intent. ✗
  • Choice C — prioritize practicality over the personal aesthetic preferences of the architect.: invokes a practicality-versus-aesthetics trade-off the passage never discusses. ✗
  • Choice D — emphasize the aesthetic distinction between nature and architecture.: contradicts the goal of connecting building and nature by suggesting curves undermine that coherence. ✗
That matches Choice A — are reminiscent of the arcing and contours found among plants and animals..
